ICPs.evidence_summary: Agency hiring patterns suggest demand for better lead capture and follow-up operations before teams scale.
Founder-led teams often delay full CRM purchases and prefer simpler workflow tools in early growth stages.

Competitors.notes: Well-known global CRM benchmark that shapes local buyer expectations even when adoption is limited.
Established CRM option likely to appear in Nepal software comparisons because of price-sensitive tiers.

Channels.rationale: Offline communities can support trust-building for B2B SaaS in a relationship-driven market.
Facebook groups remain active for operator questions and vendor recommendations in Nepal urban markets.
Search-led discovery is relevant when buyers are already comparing operational tooling.

Lead Sources.notes: Local directories can seed prospect lists for service businesses with clear contact details.

Lead Sources.lead_source: Sales and operations managers in Kathmandu startups
Lead Sources.notes: LinkedIn is a practical source for identifying operators at venture-backed or growth-stage companies.

Open Questions.question: Will Nepali-language messaging outperform English-first campaigns outside Kathmandu?
Open Questions.notes: The current workflow is urban and English-first; broader localization remains unvalidated.
